description: Add missing SDK utility documentation to docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx — both getConfig(key) (plugin settings reader) and getPreScriptValue(key) + definePreScript (pre-script APIs). Both are exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk but are not mentioned anywhere in the docs site, leaving plugin authors unable to discover these features without reading the SDK source.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Document getConfig(key) utility in SDK Utilities reference
userStories.description: Add documentation for getConfig(key) in docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx. The function is exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:253) and is the runtime reader for plugin settings that were configured via the plugin's configSchema and user-provided values in config.json. Plugin authors have no way to learn this utility exists from the docs today.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx imports `getConfig` in the top-of-file bulk import snippet (currently around line 10-23)
A new section named 'Config Utilities' or a `getConfig(key)` subsection exists and documents the signature `function getConfig(key: string): string | number | boolean | undefined`
The documentation explains that the value comes from globalThis.__openTabs.pluginConfig, which is resolved from config.json `settings.<pluginShortName>` at injection time
At least one usage example showing `const instanceUrl = getConfig('instanceUrl') as string | undefined;` appears in a `typescript` code block
The section cross-links to /docs/reference/configuration#settings for how users set values
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx (add import + new doc section + bump lastUpdated)

Context:
The plugin SDK exports `getConfig(key)` from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:253 — it reads resolved setting values from `globalThis.__openTabs.pluginConfig`, which the platform injects into the MAIN world before the adapter IIFE runs. See platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md section 'Plugin Settings' (around line 129-151). The configuration.mdx already documents the user-facing side (config.json `settings` map), so this story only needs to document the plugin-side reader. No doc page currently mentions `getConfig`.

Changes:
1. In the import block at docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx (currently lines 9-23), add `getConfig,` to the comma-separated list. The existing block lists functions like `waitForSelector`, `fetchFromPage`, `getLocalStorage`, `getPageGlobal`, etc.
2. Add a new level-2 section `## Config Utilities` (after `## Page State Utilities`, before `## Timing Utilities`). Its intro sentence should be something like: "Read resolved plugin settings that the user provided via the plugin's configSchema."
3. Under that, add a level-3 subsection `### getConfig(key)` with:
   - A short prose description
   - TypeScript signature fence: `function getConfig(key: string): string | number | boolean | undefined;`
   - A usage example showing a url-type setting read:
     ```typescript
     import { getConfig } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';
     const instanceUrl = getConfig('instanceUrl') as string | undefined;
     ```
   - A note that keys must match field names declared in the plugin's configSchema
   - A cross-link to the Configuration page (use `[Configuration](/docs/reference/configuration#settings)` or similar) for how users set values
4. Update the frontmatter line `lastUpdated: 2026-03-10` at the top of the file to `lastUpdated: 2026-04-24`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT touch anything in the actual SDK source (platform/plugin-sdk/src/*). This is a docs-only change.
- Do NOT edit docs/components/MDX.tsx (no new components are needed).
- Do NOT introduce new props, new MDX components, or new tables — match the existing style (plain prose + fenced code blocks + optional <Table> for option lists). The existing `### getPageGlobal(path)` section is a good template to mirror.
- Do NOT add react components or imports. The MDX file already uses the project's standard docComponents; any additional Table usage should import as `<Table>` (already provided by docComponents).
- Do NOT rename the import block style — keep the multi-line destructured pattern already present.

userStories.title: Document getPreScriptValue and definePreScript pre-script APIs
userStories.description: Add documentation for the pre-script feature in docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx. The feature consists of `definePreScript` (imported from the `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script` subpath only, NOT from the main barrel) and `getPreScriptValue<T>(key)` (imported from the main barrel). Pre-scripts run at document_start in MAIN world before any page scripts, letting plugins observe or patch page runtime state (auth tokens, CSRF nonces, early globals) that would otherwise be hidden before ordinary adapters load. No doc page currently mentions pre-scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx imports `getPreScriptValue` in the top-of-file bulk import snippet

A new section exists that documents `definePreScript(fn)` including the required subpath import `import { definePreScript } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script'` (and explicitly notes that importing from the main barrel is NOT supported)

The same section documents `getPreScriptValue<T>(key): T | undefined` including the three cases when `undefined` is returned (no pre-script declared, pre-script never fired for this tab, or pre-script did not call set(key, ...))

At least one end-to-end usage example shows the author writing a pre-script with `definePreScript(({ set }) => { ... set('authToken', value); ... })` AND the tool handler reading the value with `getPreScriptValue<string>('authToken')`
The documentation mentions the PreScriptContext surface: `set(key, value)`, `log` (debug/info/warn/error) and the constraint that pre-scripts have no chrome.* or main-SDK access
Frontmatter `lastUpdated` field remains updated to 2026-04-24 (set in US-001)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx (extend import block, add new doc section)

Context:
Pre-script is a documented SDK feature per platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md (see section 'Pre-Script', around line 153-201). Exports:
- `definePreScript` — exported from platform/plugin-sdk/src/pre-script-define.ts via the package.json subpath export `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script`. It is intentionally absent from the main barrel so bundlers don't pull the full SDK into the pre-script IIFE.
- `getPreScriptValue<T>(key): T | undefined` — exported from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:265 (main barrel). Reads values stashed by the pre-script at `globalThis.__openTabs.preScript[<pluginName>][key]`.

Three cases that return undefined (copy verbatim from CLAUDE.md):
1. The plugin has no pre-script declared.
2. The adapter was injected into a tab that was already open when the plugin registered (pre-scripts only fire on future navigations).
3. The pre-script ran but did not call `set(key, ...)` for this key.

Author surface (PreScriptContext): `set(key: string, value: PreScriptValue): void` stashes JSON-serializable values in the plugin's pre-script namespace (plugin name is injected at build time — plugins cannot write to each other's namespaces); `log` is a console logger with debug/info/warn/error. Pre-scripts have NO access to chrome.* APIs and NO access to the main SDK (no DOM helpers, no fetch utilities).

Changes:
1. In the import block at docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx (already modified in US-001), add `getPreScriptValue,` to the destructured list.
2. Add a new level-2 section `## Pre-Script Utilities` (placed after the new `## Config Utilities` section added in US-001, before `## Timing Utilities`).
3. Open the section with a brief explanation — pre-scripts run at document_start in MAIN world strictly before any page script, letting plugins observe or patch page runtime state (auth tokens in outbound fetch/XHR, CSRF nonces, early globals) that the page would otherwise hide before ordinary adapters load.
4. Add a level-3 subsection `### definePreScript(fn)` with:
   - IMPORTANT callout that the function MUST be imported from the subpath `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script`, never from the main barrel `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk`. Use an <Alert variant="warning"> if consistent with the docs style (see guides/plugin-development.mdx for <Alert> usage), otherwise use bold prose.
   - A signature line in a typescript fence: `function definePreScript(fn: (ctx: PreScriptContext) => void): void;`
   - A complete example: the typical auth-token interception pattern:
     ```typescript
     import { definePreScript } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script';
     
     export default definePreScript(({ set, log }) => {
       const real = window.fetch;
       window.fetch = async (input, init) => {
         const auth = (init?.headers as Record<string, string>)?.Authorization;
         if (auth) set('authToken', auth);
         return real(input, init);
       };
     });
     ```
   - A short paragraph describing the PreScriptContext shape: `set(key: string, value: PreScriptValue): void` + `log` (debug/info/warn/error). Mention that plugin name is injected at build time so plugins cannot write to each other's namespaces, and that pre-scripts have NO chrome.* access and NO main-SDK access.
5. Add a level-3 subsection `### getPreScriptValue(key)` with:
   - A signature line: `function getPreScriptValue<T>(key: string): T | undefined;`
   - An example showing the adapter reading the value:
     ```typescript
     import { getPreScriptValue } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';
     
     const token = getPreScriptValue<string>('authToken');
     ```
   - A bullet list of the 3 cases returning undefined (copy them from the Context section above verbatim — important that they appear in the docs).

Constraints:
- Do NOT add `definePreScript` to the top-of-file bulk import snippet (the one around line 10-23). That snippet imports from `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk` (the main barrel), which deliberately does not export `definePreScript`. Only `getPreScriptValue` goes there.
- Do NOT touch anything outside this single file.
- Do NOT remove or reformat other sections — the insertion must be additive.
- Do NOT claim that pre-scripts fire on every navigation — only on future navigations after the plugin registers. Tabs already open when the plugin registers will NOT have the pre-script fire (this is why undefined is a legitimate branch, not an error).
- Do NOT change the frontmatter `lastUpdated` — US-001 already bumped it to 2026-04-24, and re-writing it again is fine, but do not revert to an older date.
- The section must use the same MDX style as the rest of the page (plain prose, typescript code fences, no new imports other than what MDX.tsx already provides).
